description: Nodes for weather data ingestion and thermal-comfort analysis. Provides ERA5 meteorological loading, UTCI computation via pythermalcomfort, raster I/O via rasterio, raster zonal statistics via rasterstats, and census/polygon reprojection. Used by the 'Heterogeneous data + linked views' example workflow.
name: Weather Analysis

templates.description: Open the bundled 2022 Milan mean radiant temperature GeoTIFF and return the rasterio dataset for downstream zonal stats / UTCI computation.
templates.label: Milan MRT Loader

templates.description: Load the hourly ERA5 meteorological CSV (Td / Wind / RH per hour 'it') so a downstream UTCI step can pick the matching timestamp.
templates.label: ERA5 Milan Weather

templates.description: Load the Milan census GeoJSON (trimmed to the gt_65 column) as a GeoDataFrame so it can be reprojected and merged with UTCI.
templates.label: Milan Census Polygons

templates.description: Compute the Universal Thermal Climate Index per raster pixel using pythermalcomfort. Reads raster + weather DataFrame via the Curio MERGE_FLOW pattern (arg[0] / arg[1]) and returns the UTCI grid + shape so the zonal-stats step can rebuild it.
templates.label: UTCI from raster + weather

templates.description: Spatially join the UTCI grid into census polygons via rasterstats.zonal_stats, returning a GeoDataFrame keyed on geometry with mean UTCI + gt_65 columns. Inputs arrive in order (raster, UTCI tuple, polygons).
templates.label: UTCI Zonal Mean

templates.description: Reproject the merged census GeoDataFrame from UTM 32632 to EPSG:3395 (the projection AUTK_MAP's tile pipeline expects), drop polygons with non-positive UTCI, and tag the table with metadata.name='census' so downstream views can reference it by name.
templates.label: Reproject census to EPSG:3395

templates.description: Project the (Geo)DataFrame down to just the gt_65 column — used to feed a boxplot view that summarises the older-adult-population distribution independently of UTCI.
templates.label: Project gt_65 column
